title: AI Architect – Lilly Medicine Foundry (R5-6)
description:
At Lilly, the work is demanding because patients are waiting. We unite caring with discovery to help make life better for people around the world, knowing that every decision, every detail, and every day matters. Headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana, our over 50,000 employees around the globe take on complex challenges to discover and deliver life-changing medicines, strengthen how health is understood and managed, and support the communities we serve. This is hard, urgent, selfless work—but it’s work worth doing. If you’re driven by purpose and ready to bring your best to work that truly matters for patients, we invite you to join us.


Job Level: (R5-6) – Systems Architect

Job Family: Information Technology

Location: Hyderabad, India

Job Function: Tech@Lilly CTI-MD India

Tech Ladder (Y/N): Y

About Us

This is an opportunity you don’t want to miss!

Lilly is entering an exciting period of growth, and we are committed to delivering innovative medicines to patients around the world. LRL has increasing needs for in-house manufacture of material for clinical supplies and will therefore construct a new campus to manufacture Clinical Trial (CT) Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) to meet needs for an expanding portfolio (more and new areas), to accelerate development timelines, and to enhance supply chain robustness.

The brand-new facility also known as Lilly Medicine Foundry (Foundry) will utilize the latest technology to augment the current clinical supply chain for small molecules (SM), oligonucleotides, peptides, and Antibody Drug Conjugates (ADCs), monoclonal antibodies and bioconjugates, and add new capabilities including mRNA.

The new site will be built using the latest high-tech equipment, advanced highly integrated and automated manufacturing systems, and have a focus on minimizing the impact to our environment.

Role Overview

Lead the technical architecture strategy and implementation for The Lilly Medicine Foundry, establishing the foundational platforms, standards, and architectural patterns that enable AI solutions to scale across the site, PRD and potentially Manufacturing. This role combines deep technical expertise in AI/ML systems with strategic architecture leadership to build enterprise-grade AI capabilities that deliver measurable business value while meeting pharmaceutical industry requirements for security, compliance, and validation.

Reporting to the Director IT – CTI MD, you will provide architectural expertise and reusable platform capabilities to domain teams while establishing technical standards and governance frameworks that ensure consistency, quality, and scalability across the AI portfolio. You will partner closely with Enterprise Architecture, Data & Analytics, Infrastructure, and Security teams to integrate AI capabilities seamlessly into the Foundry’s technology ecosystem.

Primary Responsibilities

AI Architecture Strategy & Platform Design (40%)

Functional AI Architecture & Strategy

  • Define and evolve the enterprise AI architecture vision, strategy, and roadmap aligned with the Lilly Medicine Foundry and PRD objectives

  • Design reference architectures and architectural patterns for AI/ML systems spanning model development, deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management

  • Establish architectural principles and design patterns that ensure AI solutions are scalable, secure, maintainable, and compliant with pharmaceutical regulations

  • Create technology selection frameworks and architectural decision records (ADRs) to guide build vs. buy vs. partner choices

  • Drive architectural alignment across Clinical and Commercial AI initiatives to maximize reuse and minimize technical debt

AI Platform Development & Engineering

  • Design and build the core AI platform capabilities including MLOps infrastructure, model registry, feature stores, experimentation frameworks, and deployment pipelines

  • Architect solutions for AI model lifecycle management including versioning, A/B testing, monitoring, retraining, and retirement

  • Establish reusable AI services and APIs that domain teams can leverage to accelerate solution delivery (e.g., NLP services, computer vision, recommendation engines)

  • Design integration patterns for AI capabilities with Lilly's data platform, enterprise applications, and development/manufacturing systems

  • Architect hybrid cloud solutions optimizing for performance, cost, and regulatory requirements (on-premises, AWS, Azure, GCP)

Generative AI & LLM Architecture

  • Design enterprise-grade architectures for Large Language Model (LLM) deployment including prompt management, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and fine-tuning pipelines

  • Establish patterns for responsible GenAI deployment including content filtering, bias detection, hallucination mitigation, and audit trails

  • Architect solutions for domain-specific AI agents and "digital teammates" that augment development and manufacturing workflows

  • Design vector databases and semantic search capabilities to enable knowledge retrieval across Lilly's scientific and clinical content

  • Evaluate and architect solutions for frontier AI capabilities including multimodal models, agentic systems, and reasoning frameworks

Technical Standards, Governance & Compliance (30%)

AI Governance & Standards

  • Establish and maintain AI technical standards, design principles, and engineering best practices for the Foundry AI CoE

  • Create architectural review processes and governance frameworks to ensure AI solutions meet enterprise quality and compliance requirements

  • Define and enforce technical guardrails for AI development including model performance thresholds, data quality standards, and deployment criteria

  • Lead architecture review boards and provide technical guidance on complex AI initiatives across Foundry, PRD, Manufacturing

  • Develop AI solution assessment frameworks to evaluate technical maturity, scalability readiness, and production worthiness

Regulatory & Security Compliance

  • Design AI architectures compliant with pharmaceutical regulatory requirements (FDA 21 CFR Part 11, EU MDR, GxP, CSV/CSA)

  • Architect solutions meeting data privacy and security requirements (HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2, ISO 27001)

  • Establish validation and verification frameworks for AI/ML models used in regulated contexts (clinical manufacturing, product development, commercial manufacturing)

  • Design audit logging, traceability, and explainability capabilities to support regulatory submissions and inspections

  • Partner with Cyber Security, Legal, and Quality Assurance teams to ensure AI solutions meet enterprise risk management requirements

  • Architect secure data handling for synthetic data generation, anonymization, and test data management in development/manufacturing environments

AI Model Risk Management

  • Establish frameworks for AI model validation, testing, and performance monitoring in production environments

  • Design solutions for detecting and mitigating model drift, bias, fairness issues, and performance degradation

  • Architect explainability and interpretability capabilities enabling stakeholders to understand AI decision-making

  • Create incident response and rollback procedures for AI system failures or unexpected behaviors

Technical Leadership & Team Enablement (20%)

Architecture Team Leadership

  • Build and lead a team of AI/ML engineers, and platform engineers supporting the Foundry AI CoE

  • Mentor and develop architectural talent across the organization, raising technical capabilities and architecture discipline

  • Coordinate architecture resources across US and international teams ensuring consistent standards and knowledge sharing

  • Foster a culture of technical excellence, innovation, and continuous learning within the architecture function

  • Represent architecture perspective in AI CoE leadership discussions and strategic planning

Domain Team Enablement & Support

  • Provide architectural consulting and technical guidance to Development and Manufacturing domain teams building AI solutions

  • Conduct architecture workshops and design reviews helping teams apply architectural patterns and best practices

  • Create architecture documentation, playbooks, and enablement materials accelerating domain team velocity

  • Partner with enterprise AI and Architecture teams to ensure architectural alignment

  • Embed architectural thinking into agile delivery processes without creating bottlenecks

Innovation, Research & Strategic Partnerships (10%)

Emerging Technology Evaluation

  • Monitor and evaluate emerging AI technologies, frameworks, and platforms for potential adoption at Lilly

  • Conduct proof-of-concepts and technology spikes to validate new approaches before enterprise adoption

  • Maintain awareness of AI research trends in academia and industry relevant to pharmaceutical applications

  • Represent Lilly's technical perspective in evaluations of AI vendors, partners, and acquisition targets

  • Contribute to thought leadership through technical publications, conference presentations, and patent applications

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Partner with Enterprise Architecture to align AI architecture with overall technology strategy and enterprise standards

  • Collaborate with Data & Analytics teams to ensure AI solutions integrate seamlessly with data platforms and governance

  • Work with Infrastructure teams to optimize cloud and on-premises resources for AI workloads

  • Engage with Development and Manufacturing domain experts to understand business requirements and translate them into architectural solutions

  • Support business development team in technical evaluation of partnership opportunities and vendor capabilities

Minimum Qualifications

Essential Experience:

  • 5+ years in software architecture, data engineering, or ML engineering roles with at least 2 years focused on AI/ML systems architecture

  • Proven track record designing and implementing enterprise-scale AI/ML platforms and MLOps infrastructures

  • Deep expertise in modern AI/ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn), LLM architectures (transformers, RAG, fine-tuning), and GenAI technologies

  • Strong background in cloud architectures (AWS, Azure, GCP) with experience deploying AI workloads at scale

  • Experience with MLOps tools and practices (Kubeflow, MLflow, SageMaker, Vertex AI, feature stores, model registries)

  • Demonstrated success leading architecture functions in enterprise environments with complex stakeholder landscapes

  • Strong understanding of data architecture, data engineering, and data governance in AI contexts

  • Experience architecting solutions in regulated industries (healthcare, financial services, or pharmaceuticals preferred)

Preferred Experience:

  • Pharmaceutical, biotech, or healthcare industry experience with understanding of clinical development and commercial operations

  • Experience with regulatory compliance frameworks (FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP, CSV/CSA, HIPAA, GDPR)

  • Background in pharmaceutical process development, clinical or commercial pharma manufacturing

  • Experience architecting AI solutions for process development, clinical or commercial manufacturing

  • Track record building and leading distributed architecture teams across multiple geographies

  • Published research, patents, or thought leadership in AI/ML architecture domains

  • Advanced degree (MS or PhD) in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or related technical field

  • Enterprise architecture certifications (TOGAF, Zachman) or cloud certifications (AWS Solutions Architect, Azure Solutions Architect)

Required Technical Capabilities:

  • Deep understanding of machine learning algorithms, model training, evaluation, and deployment methodologies

  • Expertise in designing distributed systems, microservices architectures, and API design patterns

  • Strong knowledge of containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CloudFormation)

  • Proficiency in Python and familiarity with modern software development practices (CI/CD, testing, version control)

  • Experience with data pipeline orchestration tools (Airflow, Prefect, Dagster) and streaming technologies (Kafka, Kinesis)

  • Understanding of security architecture, identity management, encryption, and secure software development practices

  • Knowledge of vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus) and semantic search technologies for GenAI applications

Required Leadership Capabilities:

  • Strategic thinking with ability to balance innovation, pragmatism, and long-term architectural vision

  • Exceptional communication skills—ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and executives

  • Collaborative leadership style suited to federated delivery models and matrixed organizations

  • Strong influencing and negotiation skills to drive architectural standards adoption across independent domain teams

  • Systems thinking—ability to see connections across technical domains and optimize for enterprise outcomes

  • Comfort with ambiguity and ability to make pragmatic architecture decisions with incomplete information

  • Track record mentoring and developing technical talent across engineering and architecture disciplines

Location/Travel:

  • Hyderabad, India

  • 10% travel globally

Please note this is for individuals to request an accommodation as part of the application process and any other correspondence will not receive a response.

Lilly does not discriminate on the basis of age, race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

title: Assistant Manager - Grape Rd. - FT
description:
Job Objective:

To manage the daily operations of a fast-paced retail store, ensuring smooth and efficient processes across all areas, including customer service, inventory management, and sales. The role is focused on creating a positive and welcoming shopping experience for customers while maintaining a productive work environment for staff. This position reports to the Store Manager who evaluates performance annually.

In addition, embodies the Goodwill Mission to: “Empower people to achieve their most abundant life” and upholds the Company Core Values of: Relentless Pursuit of Excellence, Customer-First Mindset, Humble Confidence, Passion for Mission, and Do the Right Thing.

Supervisory Responsibilities:

  • Custodians
  • Drive Thru Ambassadors
  • Sales Production Associates
  • Textile Sales Associates

Essential Job Functions:

  • Participate in the recruitment process by identifying and hiring qualified candidates to build a competent and diverse team.
  • Develop and implement training to ensure all staff are well-versed in company policies, procedures, and customer service standards.
  • Continuously monitor employee performance, providing constructive feedback and coaching to promote professional growth and accountability.
  • Take appropriate disciplinary actions when necessary to maintain high standards of conduct and performance within the team.
  • Supervise and coordinate all store operations to ensure seamless execution of daily activities, including managing inventory, merchandising, sales floor operations, and customer service.
  • Ensure that all departments are working collaboratively to achieve business goals, optimize workflow efficiency, and provide an exceptional shopping experience for customers.
  • Establish and enforce comprehensive safety protocols to maintain a secure working environment for employees and customers.
  • Actively monitor potential security risks, including internal theft and shoplifting, and take proactive measures to mitigate such threats. Foster a culture of safety awareness and compliance among all staff members.
  • Maintain open and transparent communication with the Store Manager regarding store operations, staff performance, and any challenges that arise.
  • Participate in regular meetings to discuss store performance, share insights, and collaborate on strategies for improvement.
  • Assist in developing and implementing corrective action plans to address operational issues, ensuring that solutions are effectively executed, and outcomes are monitored for success.
  • Other duties as assigned by supervisor.

title: Senior Electrical Account Manager
description:
Overview

Cummings Electrical is hiring a Senior Electrical Project Manager in Dallas/Fort Worth, TX.

Currently seeking a Senior Electrical Project Manager to lead a team of service electricians. The SAM will oversee, guide, and manage the work of contractors, vendors, suppliers and employees. Responsibilities include efficient coordination and planning, to ensure that the team provides consistent, high-quality, cost-effective, and timely deliverables and products for multiple projects. This person will ensure that workplace health and safety requirements are met and take responsibility for promoting environmental stewardship and a work culture focused on health and safety. This position will also work closely with the company’s management team in a decision-support capacity.

Responsibilities

  • Minimum 5 years or more years of progressively responsible experience in the electrical construction industry
  • Bachelor’s degree in construction management, Engineering or related field is preferred or previous work as Licensed Electrician/Supervisor
  • Must be capable of leading a team of up to 20 electricians
  • Experience with Electrical / Power Systems such as UPS, Switchgear, Generators, PDU's; a MUST
  • Managing Projects for Electrical installation of Equipment for high profile projects is a plus
  • Customer Service Driven
  • Computer Savvy (Microsoft Suite, MS Project, etc.)
  • Solid Communicator – excellent oral and written communication skills
  • Monitor the performance and progress of staff, motivate, organize and encourage teamwork to ensure productivity targets are met

Qualifications

  • Knowledge and application of relevant safety requirements that apply to the specific work being done. Recognizes and mitigates safety hazards on the job. Observes rules and regulations to comply with personal and workplace safety standards
  • Works to create a hazard free, accident free environment
  • Establishes clear and realistic direction of organizational goals and employee roles and responsibilities
  • Maintains staff motivation and focus on goals and priorities
  • Ensures that departmental goals match those of the organization and that makes sure resources are allocated to meet priorities
  • Proven and successful track record of producing work that is highly accurate, demonstrates attention to detail and reflects well on the organization
  • Personally committed to high quality work and encourages others to have similar standards

How we support you

At Cummings, we are motivated by achieving our goals and fostering a strong work ethic. Our environment allows you to take pride in your work, create meaningful connections, and acquire growth-based development skills. We are also committed to providing you with a comprehensive range of benefits aimed at supporting your success both in and outside the workplace and ensuring your security and well-being.

Some of what we have to offer includes

  • Health benefits plan to support you, your well-being, and your mental health
  • PTO
  • 401(k) retirement plan with company contribution
  • Career growth and development opportunities
  • Regular team building/employee appreciation activities
  • On-site, fully equipped gym and locker room
  • In-house training facility
  • Weekly Pay

All applicants will be subject to background check and drug screen.Salary commensurate with experience.

title: Entry-Level Commercial HVAC Installation Technician
description:
About the Role
REIC Rentals is hiring an Entry-Level Commercial HVAC Installation Technician to join our team in Edmond, OK. This is an excellent opportunity for recent HVAC graduates or individuals looking to start a career in commercial HVAC. You'll receive hands-on training while working alongside experienced technicians to install, maintain, and service temporary climate control equipment at customer job sites and in the shop.
About Us
REIC Rentals is a trusted full-line equipment partner with 55+ locations across North America, serving the Oil & Gas, Construction, Industrial, and Event industries. We deliver high-performance equipment backed by exceptional customer service and technical expertise.
Why Join Us
  • Competitive pay with overtime opportunities
  • Company vehicle, company credit card, and per diem provided for business travel
  • Medical, dental, vision, PTO, and 401(k) with company match
  • Paid hands-on training and career advancement opportunities
  • Tuition and certification reimbursement
  • Employee referral bonus program
Travel
This position requires frequent travel to support multiple REIC Rentals locations and customer job sites across OK, KS, and TX. All business travel expenses are covered with a company vehicle, company credit card, and per diem.
Responsibilities
  • Load, transport, and unload commercial HVAC rental equipment
  • Safely drive company vehicles to customer job sites (acceptable driving record required)
  • Assist with the installation and setup of temporary HVAC equipment, including:
    • Running gas hoses
    • Installing ductwork and ventilation
    • Connecting and setting up equipment for operation
  • Assist with preventative maintenance, inspections, and basic repairs on HVAC equipment
  • Travel with experienced HVAC technicians to customer job sites throughout the assigned service area
  • Learn to troubleshoot heating, cooling, ventilation, and dehumidification equipment
  • Work closely with Commercial HVAC Service Technicians to gain hands-on field experience
  • Complete service documentation and maintain a clean, organized work area
  • Provide excellent customer service while representing REIC Rentals
  • Follow all company safety policies, procedures, and industry standards
Qualifications
  • Recent HVAC trade school graduate or equivalent technical training preferred
  • EPA Certification or the ability to obtain it (preferred)
  • Strong mechanical aptitude and willingness to learn
  • Basic knowledge of HVAC systems, electrical components, and hand tools
  • Valid driver's license with an acceptable driving record
  • Ability to lift up to 75 lbs.
  • Willingness to travel frequently
  • Must be 21 years of age or older
Build Your Career with REIC Rentals
If you're eager to launch your HVAC career, enjoy working with your hands, and want to learn from experienced technicians while traveling and gaining real-world experience, we'd love to hear from you.
#StepIntoSuccess
REIC Rentals does not accept solicitation from outside recruiters or recruiting agencies.

REIC and all affiliates operate as drug-free environments, which includes marijuana. A pre-employment drug screening that tests for THC and other drugs, as well as a full background check, is required of all newly hired employees.

description:

  • Flexibilität hinsichtlich der Arbeitszeiten einschließlich an Wochenenden und zu Abendzeiten

  • Du bist mindestens 18 Jahre alt

Bewirb Dich jetzt und werde Teil unseres Hunkemöller Lifestyles! Lade Deine aussagekräftigen Bewerbungsunterlagen inkl. Lebenslauf und relevanten Arbeitszeugnissen über das Online-Formular auf jobs.hunkemoller.de hoch.

Der erste Schritt im Bewerbungsprozess erfolgt über Bowy, unseren KI‑Chatbot, der dich via WhatsApp kontaktiert und einige Basisinformationen mit dir abstimmt. Zusätzlich erwarten dich kurze, spielerische Tests zu bestimmten Fähigkeiten.


Alle Daten werden DSGVO‑konform verarbeitet und ausschließlich für den Bewerbungsprozess genutzt. Bei Fragen sind wir gerne für dich da.

Bei Hunkemöller motivieren und bestärken wir Frauen darin, ihr schönstes und authentischstes Selbst zu sein.

Unsere Werte Light-hearted, Authentic, Curious und Empowering stehen für ein offenes, ehrliches und positives Miteinander, in dem Neugier, Vertrauen und persönliches Wachstum gefördert werden. Du liebst Fashion sowie erstklassigen Service im Einzelhandel / Verkauf und begeisterst Deine Mitmenschen mit Deiner aufgeschlossenen Art?

Als Assistant Store Manager bist Du die rechte Hand der Filialleiterin! Du gestaltest ein herausragendes Kundenerlebnis, schaffst eine inspirierende Arbeitsatmosphäre im Store und trägst maßgeblich zum Unternehmenserfolg bei.

title: Consultant Achats Confirmé - Catégorie "Aftersales Services" (SAV) F/H
description:
Nous recherchons un(e) Consultant(e) Achats confirmé(e) pour intervenir en mission longue durée chez notre client, un grand groupe international du secteur de la cosmétique.

Rattaché(e) à l'équipe dédiée aux dispositifs électroniques connectés beauté, vous pilotez de bout en bout les achats de la catégorie Aftersales Services (SAV) associée à ces appareils, sur un périmètre à dominante B2B (majoritaire face au B2C), pour l'ensemble des divisions du groupe.

Dans ce cadre, vos missions couvrent à la fois le category management et le pilotage business :

Category Management - Aftersales Services

  • Définir et mettre en œuvre la stratégie de portefeuille fournisseurs la plus adaptée ; construire et challenger les stratégies de sourcing de la catégorie ; garantir une couverture sourcing complète de la catégorie Aftersales Services
  • Piloter la market intelligence : identifier les experts internes et les acteurs de l'écosystème externe (B2B, B2C) pertinents ; conduire des analyses de couverture marché et des benchmarks à l'échelle mondiale
  • Mener les analyses de marché globales, le cost modeling et le benchmark concurrentiel en lien avec les parties prenantes
  • Identifier et maximiser les opportunités de création de valeur avec un impact mesurable sur le P&L, dans le respect de l'intégrité et de la politique anti-corruption du client
  • Négocier et gérer les contrats fournisseurs ; conduire les Business Reviews ; suivre et piloter la performance fournisseurs sur les 5 piliers : Compétitivité, Innovation, RSE, Qualité et Service
  • Mettre en œuvre les processus de gestion des risques et de prévention de la corruption conformément aux standards du groupe
  • Maintenir et améliorer en continu le "Aftersales Handbook" et les processus associés

Pilotage Business - Aftersales Services

  • Traduire les besoins business en actions de sourcing : collecter les besoins projets et les convertir en actions structurées (RFQ/RFI, sélection fournisseurs, contractualisation)
  • Construire et exécuter les projets dans le respect du budget et des délais alloués
  • Améliorer la performance fournisseurs sur les 5 piliers (Compétitivité, Innovation, RSE, Qualité, Service)
  • Contribuer à la construction budgétaire et aux mises à jour de prévisions ; assurer le suivi mensuel des dépenses par ligne de service SAV et gérer le processus de facturation associé
  • Travailler en étroite collaboration avec l'ensemble des parties prenantes de l'écosystème pour garantir la livraison des services fournisseurs conformément aux KPIs définis pour chaque projet

De formation supérieure en achats, vous justifiez d'une expérience professionnelle d'au moins 5 à 7 ans en sourcing/category management, de bout en bout.

Le premier critère recherché est votre capacité à être opérationnel(le) rapidement : autonomie, proactivité et aisance à gérer les parties prenantes dans un écosystème nouveau et complexe.

Les compétences attendues pour ce poste sont les suivantes :

  • Expérience démontrée sur les catégories de services après-vente, idéalement helpdesk, réparation ou services techniques, dans un environnement tech ou biens de grande consommation.
  • Track record confirmé en négociation et gestion de contrats de services complexes.
  • Expérience en gestion de P&L, construction budgétaire, suivi des dépenses et optimisation des coûts.
  • Expérience en Supplier Relationship Management structuré, Business Reviews et pilotage de la performance multi-fournisseurs.
  • Capacité à gérer plusieurs projets dans le respect du budget et des délais
  • Expérience dans un environnement business global ou multi-régional (B2B et/ou B2C)
  • Anglais et français courants, à l'écrit comme à l'oral

Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Chez EPSA GROUPE, nous sommes portés par la conviction que l’entreprise de demain sera responsable. Aujourd’hui, cette ambition nous amène à construire un groupe à forte valeur ajoutée humaine. Ce projet collectif est pour nous un levier de transformation, source de performance et de croissance durable. EPSA GROUPE s’engage donc dans une démarche de réflexions et d’actions de sa Responsabilité Sociale des Entreprises (RSE), et, via EPSA FOUNDATION, s’appuie sur ses expertises pour contribuer à une société plus inclusive et plus respectueuse de l’environnement.

title: Analista de Customer Service e Distribuição
description:
Junte-se a nós como Analista de Customer Service e Distribuição

PROPÓSITO DA VAGA

  • Esta função existe para atuar de forma integrada entre Customer Service, Planejamento Logístico e Operações de Transporte, garantindo uma experiência eficiente para clientes e uma execução logística consistente, com foco em nível de serviço, fluidez operacional e melhoria contínua.

  • A posição terá papel analítico e colaborativo, conectando áreas internas, operadores logísticos e parceiros de transporte para apoiar decisões operacionais, acompanhar indicadores e contribuir para a evolução dos processos da cadeia de distribuição.

Aqui, acreditamos em construir soluções práticas, sustentáveis e escaláveis — sempre colocando o cliente no centro das decisões.

Você fará parte de uma equipe que acredita em realizar um trabalho que importa — para as pessoas e para o planeta.

Porque aqui, não contratamos apenas para vagas. Convidamos você a fazer parte de algo maior.

PRINCIPAIS RESPONSABILIDADES

Gestão de Pedidos e Atendimento ao Cliente

  • Atuar como ponto de contato entre clientes, time comercial, Supply Chain e operação logística para temas relacionados a pedidos e entregas.

  • Acompanhar o fluxo da carteira de pedidos, garantindo alinhamento entre faturamento, expedição e entrega.

  • Realizar tratativas de ocorrências logísticas, recusas, devoluções, reentregas e ajustes de pedidos.

  • Apoiar reuniões internas e externas para análise de desvios, definição de ações corretivas e melhoria da experiência do cliente.

Planejamento e Operação Logística

  • Apoiar o planejamento de roteirização, consolidação de cargas e utilização da malha logística.

  • Monitorar a execução das entregas e atuar junto à torre de controle e transportadores na gestão de exceções operacionais.

  • Acompanhar disponibilidade de veículos, capacidade operacional e aderência ao plano de carregamento.

  • Contribuir para melhorias na eficiência operacional e redução de custos logísticos.

Indicadores e Análises

  • Monitorar indicadores de serviço e performance logística, como Fill Rate, OTIF, Lead Time e nível de serviço.

  • Elaborar análises, relatórios e dashboards para suporte à tomada de decisão.

  • Identificar causas raiz de desvios operacionais e apoiar planos de ação.

  • Desenvolver análises de eficiência operacional, ocupação de veículos e produtividade logística.

Melhoria Contínua e Projetos

  • Participar de iniciativas de automação, simplificação de processos e transformação digital.

  • Apoiar implementação e evolução de sistemas, ferramentas analíticas e melhorias operacionais.

  • Trabalhar em parceria com áreas de Planejamento, CD, Comercial, Tecnologia e operadores logísticos para otimizar processos e garantir fluidez operacional.

EXPERIÊNCIAS E QUALIFICAÇÕES NECESSÁRIAS

  • Ensino Superior Completo (Administração, Logística, Engenharia, Gestão de Serviços ou áreas correlatas).

  • Excel avançado

  • SAP B1

  • PowerPoint – Apresentações gerenciais

  • Programar BI (Dax), Python e VBA são diferenciais

SOFT SKILLS

  • Perfil analítico e senso crítico.

  • Capacidade de tomada rápida de decisão.

  • Organização e resiliência.

  • Boa comunicação e influência.

  • Visão do cliente e foco em excelência de atendimento.

  • Capacidade de liderar melhorias de processos e indicadores.

Na Unilever, a inclusão está no centro de tudo o que fazemos. Recebemos candidaturas de todas as origens e estamos comprometidos em criar um ambiente onde todos possam prosperar/ter sucesso. Todos os candidatos receberão consideração justa e respeitosa, e apoiamos ativamente o crescimento e desenvolvimento de cada colaborador.

INFORMAÇÕES ADICIONAIS

​​ • Ambiente dinâmico em constante transformação.

• Contato intenso com áreas internas e clientes B2B.

• Participação em projetos e iniciativas estratégicas.

Unilever is an organisation committed to equity, inclusion and diversity to drive our business results and create a better future, every day, for our diverse employees, global consumers, partners, and communities. We believe a diverse workforce allows us to match our growth ambitions and drive inclusion across the business. At Unilever we are interested in every individual bringing their ‘Whole Self’ to work and this includes you! Thus if you require any support or access requirements, we encourage you to advise us at the time of your application so that we can support you through your recruitment journey.

title: Quality Auditor - Production
description:
OVERVIEW:

Under the general supervision of the Quality Manager, the Quality Auditor - Production, performs routine tests and inspections to verify and document quality and consistency in all products manufactured in RS production facilities. As a member of the RS team, this position will support an organizational culture that cares for other's well-being through best practices in health and safety, in addition to the company's values of respect, integrity, quality, teamwork and professional growth.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Perform quality measurements and review of modules
  • Work with production operators in controlling quality
  • Maintains plant safety and help maintain plant cleanliness
  • Coordinate and maintain proper workflow
  • Document inspection results
  • Ensure non-conforming product is not used without approval
  • May need to perform tests in lab on resin
  • Assist in collecting module samples for evaluation
  • Maintain both electronic and hard copies of quality records
  • Inform Quality Manager of any discrepancies in tests or inspections
  • Communicate and work with supervisors and technical staff on production issues
  • Other duties and responsibilities as assigned by the Quality Manager

TRAINING / QUALIFICATIONS:

  • High School Diploma or equivalent experience
  • One to three years' experience within a Quality role in a production environment
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Office
  • Knowledge of Excel an asse

Physical Requirements

  • The physical requirements listed below are representative of those required to successfully perform the essential functions of this position.
  • Ability to sit, stand, and walk for extended periods throughout the workday.
  • Frequent use of hands and fingers to operate computers, equipment, and tools.
  • Frequent bending, reaching, twisting, and occasional kneeling or crouching, as required.
  • Ability to lift, carry, push, and pull up to 50 pounds.
  • Ability to wear required personal protective equipment (PPE) and work safely in the assigned work environment.

Working Conditions

  • Industrial manufacturing environment.
  • Exposure to hot and humid conditions.
  • Frequent standing, walking, bending, lifting, and other physically demanding tasks.
  • Regular interaction with heavy machinery, powered industrial equipment, and moving mechanical parts.
  • Exposure to production materials, including limited interaction with manufacturing chemicals while following all required safety procedures and PPE requirements.

What's in It for You

  • Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage through Cigna.
  • 401(k) retirement plan with a company match of up to 5%.
  • Paid time off and company holidays.
  • A collaborative team environment focused on safety, quality, and continuous improvement.

Why RS?

At RS Technologies, we are redefining the future of utility infrastructure. Our composite utility poles are stronger, lighter, and longer lasting than traditional materials, helping utilities build more resilient and sustainable electrical grids worldwide.

Equal Opportunity Employer

RS Technologies is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where every employee is treated with respect and given the opportunity to succeed. We

title: Sales Associate / Jewelry Consultant - Peoples Jewellers - Centerpoint Mall - Toronto, ON
description:
We have many opportunities available on our other career site pages. Click here to link to our careers page!

Peoples Jewellers is a people-first company, and we recognize that our jewellery consultants are every bit as dynamic and brilliant as the jewellery we offer. They are the reason Peoples Jewellers has become the largest retailer of fine jewellery in Canada, with over 90 locations from the Maritimes to British Columbia. Peoples Jewellers is part of Signet Jewelers, a purpose-driven company who believes love inspires love. Signet is also “Great Place to Work-Certified”™.

Peoples Jewellers is now hiring SEASONAL, PART-TIME, and FULL-TIME team members!

Do you thrive on building personal relationships that will last a lifetime? Join our team today to help our customers celebrate life and express love!

Your role at Peoples Jewellers:

As a part of our Sales Team you are responsible for providing a superior experience to our valued customers. In addition, you will:

  • Engage customers in conversation to understand their needs and desires
  • Ability to present merchandise and share detailed information regarding features and benefits of products
  • Provide information regarding extended service plans and financing options
  • Meet individual and team sales goals

We think you’d be great for this role if you have:

  • A desire to help our customers celebrate the special moments in their lives
  • Strong customer service, sales, retail and/or jewelry experience
  • Flexible availability to work during “peak” retail hours such as nights, weekends, and holidays
  • A positive, customer -focused approach in delivering an exceptional customer experience
  • Strong communication and relational skills

We put our People First by offering the following benefits:

  • Base pay, $17.60 – $21.90 plus commission on sales. Final pay rate shall be determined and is based on experience and qualifications
  • Medical, dental, vision and prescription insurance (full-time team members)
  • Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP)
  • Paid Time Off (full-time and part-time team members)
  • Paid holidays (full-time team members)
  • Tuition reimbursement, including DCA courses based on position
  • Training — Associate Training System, Management Training System, District Manager in Training, career development and more
  • Merchandise discounts
  • Incentive trips and contests

Peoples Jewellers is part of the Signet Jewelers family and is an equal opportunity employer committed to promoting inclusion and diversity for all employees. We value integrity, teamwork and offer opportunities for advancement as part of our promote from within philosophy.

Please know that while we appreciate every applicant's interest in joining our team, we can only contact those selected for further consideration.

Don't forget, we have many opportunities available on our other career site pages. Click here to link to our careers page!

title: Installation Project Manager
description:
WE ARE
Renewal by Andersen of Central Virginia is looking for a Installation Project Manager to join our team. We are looking for an enthusiastic, motivated individual who has strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills. The ideal candidate will be able to communicate effectively with both internal and external stakeholders, utilize customer service and time management skills, and can prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment. If this sounds like you, then we want to hear from you!

OUR CULTURE
Our focus is people. Every member of our team is committed to taking care of not only our customers and the community we serve, but each other. We have a culture of making a difference, and we live those values every day. By taking care of ourselves and each other, we can take better care of our customers. It is our goal to bring out the best in everyone. We are committed to having an inclusive and diverse workplace where all people feel respected, valued and driven to realize their full potential.

POSITION PURPOSE
The Installation Project Manager will be responsible for capturing accurate measurements for window and door replacements, identifying all secondary materials for each project, and meeting customer expectations.

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING
    • Apply sound installation practices and accepted measurement techniques to install windows and doors for every fenestration, for an average of 60 projects per month.
    • Ensures all projects have been sold in a manner commensurate with company policies and financial objectives.
    • Determine work flow, project complexity and identifications of appropriate skills necessary to complete a project.
    • Provide complete installation methods and materials estimate for jobs, ensuring each job meets financial, construction and manufacturing standards.
    • Management of the installation life cycle, including the direction of subcontractors, and decisions affecting the financial outcome of the project.
    • Processing of project related paperwork and other administrative duties, including EPA lead safe work practice documentation when applicable.
    • This position requires travel to Richmond, VA, for training, team meetings, and company events. Travel to RVA will be more frequent during the first three months of onboarding.

WHAT YOU BRING

    • Experience in construction trade, preferably window and door installation
    • Familiarity with hand and power tools
    • High School graduate or trade school
    • Must be humble, hungry, and people-smart
    • Strong proficiency using a measuring tape
    • Exceptional customer service skills.
    • Ability to navigate iPad and have basic computer knowledge
    • Experience working with Salesforce or similar programs

WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?

    • Be part of a great team and company with 100+ years in the window and door industry
    • Competitive salary with ROBUST quarterly bonus potential!
    • We offer a full complement of employee benefits including health, dental, vision, life, 401k, and more!
    • Paid time off!
    • Opportunities for Advancement
    • Company vehicle and gas card
    • The necessary tools to perform at the highest level

COMPENSATION

  • Starting Pay: $70,000/annually

THE RENEWAL BY ANDERSEN STORY

Renewal by Andersen is the full-service window-replacement division of 115-year-old Andersen Corporation, the owner of the most trusted family of window brands in America. RbA was founded with a mission to redefine the industry and to offer a different-and better-window-replacement experience. Renewal by Andersen has seven company-owned locations, and over 100 independently owned "affiliates" across the United States.

title: Senior - Tax CIT
description:
About B S R & Co. LLP

B S R & Co. LLP is a member of the B S R & Affiliates network of firms registered with the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India. B S R & Co LLP has its presence in 14 cities in India with over 120 partners and an employee strength of over 5,000 professionals. The firm is engaged in audit, other assurance and taxation services. The firm has a client base spanning Indian businesses, multinationals, and listed companies in India across industry sectors. The endeavor is to continuously keep raising the bar in terms of audit quality, experience for our people, and adoption of better technology.

 TempHtmlFile

About B S R & Co. LLP
‎
‎ B S R & Co. LLP is a member of the B S R & Affiliates network of firms registered with the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India. B S R & Co LLP has its presence in 14 cities in India with over 120 partners and an employee strength of over 5,000 professionals. The firm is engaged in audit, other assurance and taxation services. The firm has a client base spanning Indian businesses, multinationals, and listed companies in India across industry sectors. The endeavor is to continuously keep raising the bar in terms of audit quality, experience for our people, and adoption of better technology .

Roles and Responsibilities

 Adhering to clients’ corporate tax compliance timelines as per statute (assessments, filing of returns, etc.)

 Engaging in corporate tax mandates for clients

 Engage in technical research on latest case laws and circulars

 Preparing memos & presentations for advisory assignment

 Assistance in preparing submissions for the clients for scrutiny assessments, rectification / stay application, appeal to be filed with the Commissioner of Income-tax and Income-tax Appellate Tribunal

 Assistance in representation before the Tax authorities and following up with the tax authorities for rectification / stay applications / refund orders of the clients

 Assistance in review of tax provisioning and tax audit finalization for audit clients

 Understanding of various tax treaties and application of relevant articles of the treaty in finalising tax returns / advisory engagements.

 Developing and demonstrating strong technical knowledge

 Ensure timely billing is done for the clients and Ensure client trackers, billing trackers, are upto date and provide real time updates

 Follow ups with clients for timely payment and TDS certificates

 Compliance with firm's internal risk management policies and client administration

 Assisting in preparing letter of engagement with the clients and ensuring timely preparation of time spent for the clients and invoicing and collection

 Ensure proficiency with firm's internal tools and technologies to upload client deliverables, etc

 Assist in filing /document management from an internal review perspective.

 Coaching/ developing junior members on the concepts of corporate tax

 Provide assistance to seniors in business development

 Making presentation on current topics in internal trainings

 Attend external trainings and keep updated with the latest developments

 To actively participate in Firm's CSR activities

Job Specifications :

Qualifications

Qualified CA

Skills

• Strong analytical ability

• Excellent communication and presentation skills

• Client relationship management

• Commercial awareness

• Team player

• Experience in working in a regulatory environment and exposure in corporate tax, corporate law, FEMA and SEBI would be an advantage

Experience

 0-2 years of experience in direct tax/corporate Tax

B S R & Co LLP has its presence in 14 cities in India with over 120 partners and an employee strength of over 5,000 professionals. The firm is engaged in audit, other assurance and taxation services. The firm has a client base spanning Indian businesses, multinationals, and listed companies in India across industry sectors. The endeavor is to continuously keep raising the bar in terms of audit quality, experience for our people, and adoption of better technology.

title: Bench Jeweler - Signet Jewelers - Arrowhead Towne Center - Glendale, AZ
location: Design and Service Center - Arrowhead Towne Center - Glendale, AZ
description:
We have many opportunities available on our other career site pages. Click here to link to our careers page!

Join our Field Design & Service Center Team! Whether you are just starting your career or an experienced Jeweler, we have plenty of opportunities for your growth and success. Start as an Apprentice Jeweler completing administrative/clerical tasks and polishing jewelry, and work toward becoming a Bench Jeweler. We are also looking for candidates with knowledge of stone setting, sizing, polishing and jewelry repair. Working with our team gives you the opportunity to advance from beginner to artisan in a unique promote-from-within environment.

title: The North Face: Seasonal Sales Associate - Pleasant Prairie
location: USCA > USA > Wisconsin > Pleasant Prairie 073 - TNF
description:
At The North Face we dare to lead the world forward through Exploration. We were born to Explore, Disrupt and to Lead. We were born to Love Wild Places and to Spark Curiosity. We believe that exploration is a mindset - both on the mountain and off the mountain - and it infuses everything we do. As a community of explorers, we stay curious about new ideas, places, and people.

So, before we get to the job details, take a minute to learn a little more about us – our values and our culture. If you can see yourself working side-by-side with a team of adventure-loving people, The North Face just may be the place for you.

To learn more about our values and our culture, visit The North Face Careers or www.thenorthface.com.

Seasonal Sales Associate

What will you do?

A day in the life of a Seasonal Sales Associate at The North Face looks a little like this.

As a Sales Associate, you are the face of the brand—bringing energy, authenticity, and a customer-first mindset to every interaction. You will create memorable shopping experiences by engaging with customers, sharing product knowledge, and delivering exceptional service. As part of a collaborative team, you’ll contribute to the store’s success through sales, visual merchandising, and operational excellence. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced retail environment, enjoys connecting with people, and is passionate about delivering best-in-class customer experience. Let’s break down that day-in-the-life a bit more.

  • Deliver a high level of personalized customer engagement through genuine interactions.​
  • Build sales by maintaining a consumer-centric mindset and modeling selling behaviors.​
  • Share relevant product, brand, and community knowledge to enhance the customer experience.​
  • Achieve individual and team sales goals by utilizing available resources and tools.​
  • Assist in visual merchandising, product replenishment, and maintaining brand presentation standards.​
  • Perform cashier duties and support loss prevention initiatives as needed.​
  • Maintain a clean, organized, and customer-ready store environment.​
  • Adhere to all company policies, procedures, and safety standards.​
  • Model inclusive behavior that respects diverse backgrounds and experiences.​

*This position will support the store during peak season with potential to transition to regular employment*

What do you need to succeed?

We all bring unique skills to work each day. For this role, there are foundational skills needed to succeed, along with 0–1 year of relevant experience. A High School Diploma or equivalent is preferred; but we value hands-on experience and demonstrated achievements most.

The foundation skills you will need in this position are:

  • Ability to genuinely and comfortably engage with a diverse group of customers.​
  • Strong communication skills—both verbal and written.​
  • Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team and adapt to changing business needs.​
  • Customer service experience preferred; retail experience is a plus.​
  • Willingness to learn and share product and brand knowledge.​
  • Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.​
  • Proficient computer skills including basic retail systems and applications.

title: Business Development Specialist
description:
Overview

Cummings Electrical is hiring a Business Development Specialist for our Strategic Accounts in Houston, TX.

Cummings Electrical is one of the country's fastest growing and most respected electrical contractors. Headquartered near Dallas-Fort Worth airport, our projects span the state and nation. Since 2002, Cummings has maintained consistent growth, and we believe the future is bright! We strive to make being part of the Cummings team so interesting, purposeful, and fulfilling that working anywhere else would be a letdown. Our core values include building solid partnerships, providing exceptional service, nurturing a positive work environment, and offering comprehensive support to all our teammates.

Responsibilities

  • Generate Leads and create lasting partnerships
  • Negotiate, prepare, and submit all contracts with prospective clients
  • Determine pricing schedules for quotes, promotions, and negotiations
  • Coordinate and sales efforts with promoting company programs and marketing strategies

Qualifications

  • 3 years experience
  • Competency in Microsoft applications including Word, Excel, and Outlook
  • Able to work comfortably in a fast-paced environment (industrial experience a plus)
  • Ability to prepare weekly and monthly reports
  • Comfortable giving sales presentations to a range of prospective clients

How we support you:

Our environment allows you to take pride in your work, create meaningful connections, and acquire growth-based development skills.

We are also committed to providing you with a comprehensive range of benefits aimed at supporting your success both in and outside the workplace and ensuring your security and well-being.

Some of what we have to offer includes:

  • Weekly Pay
  • Bonuses
  • Job advancement
  • Flexible work schedule
  • Health benefit options
  • 401(k) company contribution
  • Regular team building/employee appreciation activities
  • On-site, fully equipped gym

Cummings Electrical is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Store Manager - Zales - Stonewood Center Mall
location: Zales - Stonewood Center Mal - Downey, CA
description:
We have many opportunities available on our other career site pages. Click here to link to our careers page!

You are a diamond and Zales celebrates that fact! We recognize that every one of our jewelry consultants has a unique sparkle, and we find ways to empower them to help our customers Celebrate Life and Express Love. Zales is part of Signet Jewelers, a purpose-driven company who believes love inspires love. Signet is also “Great Place to Work-Certified”™. There are brilliant career paths awaiting you – rewarding opportunities to impact the lives of others and inspire love. Join us!

STORE MANAGER

Title: Store Manager

Reports To: District Manager

Reporting to this Position: Assistant Store Manager, Jewelry Consultant, Sales Support and Inventory Control

Position Summary:

Reporting to the District Manager (DM), the Store Manager (SM) will lead the day-to-day operations of a single store to achieve all sales, profitability, customer satisfaction, inventory control, brand representation, and team member growth and development to maximize the results of the store. Through personal selling, leadership, and coaching, the SM will champion a People First store environment that promotes diversity and inclusion, the development of life-time relationships with customers and the delivery of a best-in-class customer experience.

Vision and Purpose:

Communicates performance expectations and ensures all team members understand the impact of their performance on company success. Coaches and develops the store staff to instill understanding of brand image and to ensure consistent representation of the brand in customer interactions and execution of store visuals and marketing. Supports and communicates the strategic relevance of corporate initiatives and executes corporate plans to produce desired results. Asks questions to ensure full understanding of strategy and process and ensures staff understands how the goals will be achieved. Demonstrates the highest level of professionalism to customers and team members by displaying integrity and honesty at all times, and by conducting himself/herself in a manner that will influence and earn the respect of team members and management. Displays a positive and enthusiastic attitude so as to inspire and motive others to achieve and attain goals.

Customer Obsession:

Responsible for ensuring team members, and self, deliver an exceptional customer experience every time through training, customer outreach, communication and clear expectations. Empowers team members to respond to and resolve customer issues within established parameters and drives a high level of customer service through follow-up and customer outreach

Compiles and follows up on customer requests.

Critical Thinking & Innovative Action:

Attains sales projections and sales quotas as set by the Company and develops strategies to maximizes both personal and team members sales potential while achieving goals. Observes operations to identify successes and opportunities for improvement. Recognizes strengths and identifies root cause of operational problems. Coaches the store staff to address and correct. Reviews and analyzes reports. Identifies trends, opportunities and available resources and develops plans to address issues. Keeps management informed of the marketing, merchandising, and payment programs of the competitors within the area and advises management new or changing merchandise and services needs and excesses. Implements initiatives that will change behaviors to produce results. Works with Signet SMs in the same mall or general vicinity to foster an environment of open communication, collaboration, efficient use of resources, and accomplishment of shared objectives. Demonstrates an ability to motivate, persuade, and influence the actions of others.

Employee Experience:

Demonstrates a commitment to the Company’s People First vision. Responsible for the selection and development of talent to drive store growth. Provides a compelling onboarding experience and ongoing coaching and development in the areas of operations, sales techniques, customer service, product knowledge, inventory control and visual merchandising. Communicates performance expectations, sets actionable goals for self and others and monitors progress and performance against desired behaviors. Maintains open communication with team members and management, encouraging participation and idea sharing. Maintain accuracy of team member data in system records to ensure team members receive eligible Total Rewards.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion:

Responsible for establishing a positive, professional work atmosphere in which team members are able to bring their full self to work and want to do their best. Ensure all team members, guests and vendors are treated with respect and valued for their contribution. Demonstrates the ability to communicate, understand, and empathize with others.

Performance Excellence:

Responds promptly and accurately to all management directives, requests for merchandise transfers, inventories, and request for information. Maintains the neatness and cleanliness of the location to represent the banner. Responsible for compliance with Company policies and practices. Conducts himself/herself in a manner that will merit the goodwill and respect of customers and fellow employees. Achieve outstanding sales performance and increase profit margins by controlling discounting, expenses, payroll, and repair departments. Work a schedule based on the right time to effectively run your business including a combination of both opening and closing shifts, weekday and weekends dependent on operating hours, sales requirements, customer traffic, proper store coverage and time to coach, train and develop your team. Typical hours should aim for a five-day, 40-hour work week during non-peak hours. Hours will increase during peak selling periods.

  • Performs additional duties and projects as assigned.
  • Consistent regular scheduled attendance is considered an essential function of this job.

Work Schedule:

  • During non-peak periods, managers should aim for a five-day, 40-hour work week.
  • Schedule based on the right time to effectively run your business.
  • A combination of both opening and closing shifts should be scheduled throughout the week, keeping in mind:
    • Operating hours, sales requirements, customer traffic, proper store coverage and time to coach, train and develop your team.

NOTE: Hours will increase during peak selling periods.

Position Qualifications:

  • Education Required: Minimum of 2 years of college preferred
  • Required or Acceptable Job-Related Experience: at least 3 years of retail sales experience, OR 2 years retail sales experience plus a minimum of 1-year retail management experience.
  • Preference will be given to candidates with specialty retail or jewelry experience
  • Years of Job-Related Experience Required: at least 3 years of retail sales experience, OR 2 years retail sales experience plus a minimum of 1-year retail management experience.
  • Preference will be given to candidates with specialty retail or jewelry experience.
  • Preference will be given to candidates that are Diamond Council of America (DCA) certified.
  • Technical/Other Skills Required: Advanced Communication skillset, both written and verbal.
  • Proficient with computers, with advanced proficiency in MS Office and other related business, technology, and communication tools.
  • Strong interpersonal skills to build effective employee and customer relationships.
  • Advanced presentation skills to provide effective team member training and excellent customer presentations.
  • Strong problem solving and conflict management skills to support internal and external customers.
  • Ability to present themselves in a professional manner while communicating via email, social media, video conference, telephonic and text.
  • Ability to plan and organize tasks to meet deadlines and expectations.
  • Ability to work independently without immediate supervision.
  • Ability to effectively set priorities, develop a plan to meet goals and expectations and to effectively delegate to others for the success of the team.
  • Ability to understand and thoroughly explain detailed information.
  • Reliable and dependable.

Additional Language(s) Required: Bilingual skills are a plus.

Physical Demands:

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to communicate with and comprehend others. This job regularly requires the employee to stand, to reach with hands and arms, and to move between display cases to handle and/or feel merchandise. The employee is also required to sit, stoop, bend, kneel or crouch as needed. The employee must be able to lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. This job requires visual acuity sufficient to discern differences in quality of gemstones with or without the aid of a jeweler’s loupe. Standard workweek regularly includes weekends, evenings, holiday, and extended hours. Travel to training sessions and business meetings including air travel and overnight travel may be required.

Base pay, $24.04 – $39.09 plus commission on sales. Final pay rate shall be determined and is based on experience and qualifications.

Signet Jewelers is an equal opportunity employer committed to promoting diversity of all levels of employees. Please know that while we appreciate every applicant's interest, we can only contact those selected for further consideration.

We particularly encourage women and minorities who are interested in management opportunities at Signet to participate in this program and use this online registry system to express your interest in a management position. Registration in this online system does not guarantee a promotion, but is necessary for consideration for any promotional opportunity to a management position listed in this registry.

Note: For internal candidates, please complete your Professional Profile in Workday by clicking on your picture or the cloud then select “Job” and “Professional Profile”. Enter your entire job history, education, skills and internal projects. To add your Military Service and tenure, select “Personal” and click “Edit.”

title: Chef d'équipe logistique H/F
description:
Le domaine de la logistique vous passionne, alors découvrez cette offre, il s'agit probablement de votre futur emploi!

Rattaché(e) à votre responsable d'exploitation, vous supervisez votre activité (préparation, réception, expédition ou contenants).

Organisé(e) et réactif(ve), vous assurez la gestion quotidienne de votre équipe en garantissant la sécurité des biens et des personnes ainsi que la propreté de votre zone. Vous favorisez également l'application et l'évolution des bonnes pratiques de travail.

Vous vous assurez enfin de la disponibilité des moyens nécessaires à la bonne réalisation de la performance de votre équipe. Vous effectuez le suivi et le reporting de vos indicateurs en proposant des plans d'optimisation.

Véritable "manager", vous intégrez, accompagnez et motivez avec bienveillance vos collaborateurs. A l'écoute de votre équipe, vous établissez des relations de confiance propices à son épanouissement et à la réalisation des objectifs.

De formation Bac+2 (BTS, DUT logistique ou équivalent), vous justifiez de 3 années d'expérience minimum en management d'équipes. La maîtrise de la bureautique est impérative (WMS, Pack office, Infolog est un plus)

Autonome et rigoureux(se), vous savez vous adapter aux imprévus au sein d'un environnement stimulant et en constante évolution.

Exemplaire et bon(ne) communiquant(e) vous savez également arbitrer et prendre des décisions.

Enfin, homme/femme de terrain vous aimez le challenge et savez encourager et promouvoir les réussites collectives en insufflant un esprit de service client.

Seules les compétences font la différence. Le Groupement Mousquetaires s'engage activement en faveur de la diversité, en créant un environnement de travail inclusif qui garantit l'égalité des opportunités pour chacun(e).

location: Design and Service Center - Eastgate Power Center - Henderson, NV
description:
We have many opportunities available on our other career site pages. Click here to link to our careers page!

Join our Field Design & Service Center Team! Whether you are just starting your career or an experienced Jeweler, we have plenty of opportunities for your growth and success. Start as an Apprentice Jeweler completing administrative/clerical tasks and polishing jewelry, and work toward becoming a Bench Jeweler. We are also looking for candidates with knowledge of stone setting, sizing, polishing and jewelry repair. Working with our team gives you the opportunity to advance from beginner to artisan in a unique promote-from-within environment.

Signet Jewelers, the world’s largest specialty retail of diamond jewelry, is currently seeking entry-level Jeweler Apprentices to join our Field Design & Service Center Teams! As you begin your career as a Jeweler Apprentice, we have plenty of opportunities for your growth and success. Signet’s Design & Service Centers offer a full training program to elevate your skills from Apprentice to Jeweler.

As an Apprentice, you will learn the business from the ground up, this will entail full administrative responsibilities, including customer service, interacting with both our stores and customers, completing administrative/clerical tasks, photography of jewelry for intake/tracking and learning the basics of jewelry repair starting with polishing and quality control, all while you train toward becoming a Bench Jeweler. While we are seeking candidates with any basic jewelry or jewelry repair knowledge, we will also consider any candidate that has a passion and desire to learn the jeweler trade. Working with our team gives you the opportunity to advance from apprentice to artisan in a promote-from-within environment as you progress through our unique training journey.

Our Design & Service Center Team Members are dedicated to delivering a Customer -First experience that exceeds our customers’ expectations for custom jewelry design and repairs.

Desired Administrative Skills:

  • Customer Service minded with strong interpersonal skills
  • High standard for quality work
  • Basic Computer Skills
  • Excellent Organizational Skills
  • Detail Orientated
  • Knowledge of Shipping/Receiving
  • Working in a fast-paced environment
  • Multi-tasking & time management

Position Requirements:

  • High school degree or equivalent preferred but not required
  • Some knowledge of jewelry or jewelry repair/design preferred but not required
  • Varying hours including some nights and weekends
  • Full-time consists of a 40-hour work week with seasonal required overtime

A Sampling of Our Total Rewards:

  • Minimum starting rate of $15 per hour. Base pay offered may vary depending on geographic region, internal equity, job related knowledge, skills and experience, among other factors.
  • Jeweler Certification classes to develop and enhance your bench skills
  • Benefits including medical, dental, vision and prescription insurance (full-time Team Members)
  • 401(k) with company match
  • Competitive Pay
  • Paid Time Off (PTO)
  • Paid holidays (full-time Team Members)

  • Merchandise discounts

Signet Jewelers has over 220 Design & Service Centers throughout the United States, located within all Jared Jewelers and select Kay Jewelers and Zales stores.

title: Radiography Assistant
description:
Radiographer Assistant - Bon Secours Hospital Cork

Due to expansion of services, Bon Secours Hospital Cork are seeking a Radiographer Assistant to join highly regarded Diagnostic Imaging Department on a permanent basis. This role offers an excellent opportunity to be part of a progressive, patient-centred imaging service within a leading private hospital.

About the Role
The fundamental philosophy guiding this position will be to promote and preserve the Bon Secours mission, values (Respect, Justice, Compassion, Integrity, Innovation, Quality and Stewardship) and ethos by effective delivery of mission objectives in your capacity as a Radiography Assistant.

Duties and responsibilities:

Under the general direction and control of the Radiographic Services Manager, the Radiography Assistant will be responsible for the following:

Clinical Duties

Will assist the Radiographers and other staff, primarily in Ultrasound, Specialist Breast Care Centre and Computed Tomography areas as requested. This includes preparation of lists, delivering contrast to wards and preparation for scans and IT work for reception duties and insurance claim forms for patients and preparing patients for imaging.

  • Manage Radiology Information System list and patient flows as required.
  • Follow instructions, carry out tasks and adhere to safety procedures, as directed by the Radiographer, in line with the department and hospital policies.
  • Liaise with the Radiographer, regarding patient’s attendance in the department.
  • To observe patients and report any concerns back to the Radiographer.
  • Attend other departments for training / observation on initial appointment and at intervals as directed by the Radiographic Services Manager.
  • Attend and participate in meetings and in-service training when recommended.
  • Assist with moving and handling of patients.
  • Assist patients with dressing /undressing if required.
  • Transfer and accompany or direct patients to and from the department if required.
  • Prepare the patients and environment for examinations as directed by the Radiographer.
  • Decontamination of ultrasound probes.

General Duties

  • To assist in maintaining order, cleanliness and tidiness of procedure rooms and changing cubicles in accordance with hospital infection control and department guidelines.
  • Cleaning of radiology equipment as required.
  • Maintain procedure rooms in a clean and safe condition.
  • To assist in managing department stock, ordering and receiving supplies, monitoring equipment and reporting faulty equipment as outlined by local departmental policies.
  • Be familiar with Fire and Health & Safety regulations and ensure safety of self and others during the course of duty.
  • To deal with emergency situations as appropriate.
  • To comply with department protocols regarding documentation and recording statistical data.
  • To assist where necessary certain administrative tasks as designated by the Radiographer, including answering telephone and recording messages, filing etc.
  • To perform such other duties as may be assigned by the Radiographic Services Manager.

Essential Criteria:

  • Leaving Certificate
  • Flexible
  • Good Communicator
  • Innovation
  • Adaptable
  • Team Spirit

Desirable Criteria:

  • Certificate from relevant area
  • Previous experience in a similar environment

For informal enquiries, please contact Ciaran Walsh, Radiography Manager, at cewalsh@bonsecours.ie or Grainne Curran, Radiographer, gcurran@bonsecours.ie


To apply, please submit your application via our website / Workday. Closing Date: August 7th, 2026


Bon Secours Health System is an equal opportunities employer. Guided by our values of Human Dignity, Compassion, Stewardship, Service, and Integrity, we are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive workplace where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, abilities, and perspectives, and we provide reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process.

title: Sales Administrator (Embassy Suites Brier Creek, Raleigh, NC)
description:
Sales Administrator

Department: Sales & Marketing
Reports To: Director of Sales & Marketing

Position Summary
The Sales Administrator plays a vital role in supporting the Sales & Marketing team at Embassy
Suites and Hampton Inn & Suites RDU | Brier Creek by providing administrative, operational,
and customer service support that contributes to achieving the hotel’s revenue goals. This
position assists with group sales, corporate accounts, catering coordination, client
communications, reporting, and sales systems while ensuring exceptional hospitality and
adherence to Hilton and Winwood brand standards.

The Sales Administrator serves as the primary administrative resource for the Sales Department
and works closely with the Front Office, Catering, Food & Beverage, Revenue Management,
Housekeeping, and Accounting to ensure an outstanding experience for guests and clients.

Essential Job Functions

Administrative Support
 Provide comprehensive administrative support to the Director of Sales & Marketing and
Sales Managers.
 Prepare proposals, contracts, group resumes, and correspondence for prospective and
existing clients.
 Maintain organized electronic and paper filing systems in accordance with Hilton
policies.
 Schedule appointments, client site visits, and internal meetings.
 Answer departmental telephone calls and respond promptly to guest and client inquiries.
Sales & Group Coordination
 Manage incoming group inquiries and distribute leads appropriately.
 Enter and maintain accurate account, contact, and booking information in Hilton sales
systems.
 Prepare contracts, rooming list documentation, and event-related paperwork.
 Monitor contract status, deposit due dates, cutoff dates, and important client milestones.
 Coordinate site inspections, familiarization tours, and client hospitality arrangements.
 Assist with group arrivals by communicating details with Operations and Front Office
teams.

Hilton Systems & Reporting
 Maintain accurate records within Hilton systems, including:
o Delphi.fdc
o PEP Property Management System
o Hilton systems for lead management and reporting

 Generate weekly production reports, sales activity reports, pace reports, and monthly
departmental statistics.
 Maintain account production information and sales databases.
 Assist with preparation of annual business plans and monthly forecasting information.
Client & Internal Communication
 Serve as a liaison between clients and hotel departments.
 Coordinate guest requests with Front Office, Housekeeping, Food & Beverage,
Engineering, and Accounting.
 Ensure all group requirements are communicated accurately and timely.
 Provide prompt follow-up on client requests before, during, and after events.
Marketing Support
 Assist with email campaigns, promotional materials, and social media initiatives.
 Coordinate collateral inventory and hotel promotional items.
 Support community events, chamber functions, trade shows, and networking
opportunities.
 Help maintain digital and printed sales materials consistent with Hilton brand standards.
Brand Standards & Compliance
 Maintain compliance with Embassy Suites and Hilton brand standards.
 Protect confidential guest and company information.
 Support Hilton’s culture of hospitality, integrity, teamwork, ownership, and excellence.
 Participate in required Hilton training programs and departmental meetings.
